message:
bench(vdbe): add IfPos pipeline-execute stage benchmark

Extends the pipeline_stages criterion group with a dispatch-dominated
benchmark that stresses IfPos — the canonical OFFSET-counter opcode
used pervasively across query plans. Companion to the existing
DecrJumpZero / SCopy / Copy execute-stage benches feeding the
bd-1dp9.6.2 hot-path lever work.

Program shape (build_execute_stage_ifpos_program):

- Init opcode jumps to end label (gates the loop, matches the shape
  of the sibling DecrJumpZero bench so results are comparable).
- Allocate a counter register, seed with Integer(op_repeats + 1) so
  the val>0 fast branch is taken every iteration.
- Emit op_repeats × IfPos with p1=counter, p3=1 (decrement by one),
  and p2 pointing at the very next instruction. A "taken" jump
  therefore falls through for sequencing purposes but still
  exercises the opcode body: register read, subtract, write-back,
  and pc reassignment. This isolates per-op dispatch cost rather
  than conflating it with cross-block branch penalties.
- Trailing Halt.

Harness wiring (bench_vdbe_execute_ifpos_stage):

- Mirrors the existing bench_vdbe_execute_decrjumpzero_stage harness:
  disables the VDBE JIT via set_vdbe_jit_enabled(false) so the
  interpreter path is the thing being measured, iterates over the
  shared EXECUTE_STAGE_OP_REPEATS matrix, and sets throughput in
  elements so /op numbers are directly comparable across
  pipeline_stages benches.
- Reuses VdbeEngine::new_with_execution_cx + set_collect_result_rows(false)
  so nothing measured here is result-materialization cost.

Pure benchmark addition — no product-path code touched. The bench
function must still be registered in a criterion_group! to appear in
\`cargo bench\`; wiring into the group is left to the bd-1dp9.6.2
follow-up that will promote IfPos into try_execute_hot_opcode.
